palette_hint: Background #0f1117 (near-black), body text #c9d1d9 (cool light gray), primary accent #58a6ff (electric blue), secondary accent #3fb950 (mint green) for success/positive states.
type_style: Headings in system-ui bold (700 weight) at tight line-height for technical authority; body in 'Courier New', monospace at 14px for code blocks; prose in system-ui regular 400 at 1.6 line-height.
description: FerroFlux Systems is a fictional real-time magnetic sensor data pipeline SDK with full reference documentation covering installation, REST/WebSocket API endpoints, YAML configuration schemas, version history, and diagnostic guides.
constraints: Fixed left sidebar 260px wide containing collapsible nav sections (Installation, Core Concepts, SDK Methods, Config Schema); main content area fills remaining width with max-width 860px and padding 40px 48px; sidebar background #161b22, border-right 1px solid #30363d.
Top header bar is sticky at height 56px, background #0f1117, border-bottom 1px solid #30363d, containing logo 'FerroFlux' in #58a6ff bold and a breadcrumb trail in #8b949e.

API reference page uses a two-column layout inside main: left column 65% for description/parameters table, right column 35% with sticky code-sample panel showing example curl requests in a #0d1117 code block with #3fb950 syntax highlights.
All data tables use border-collapse, header row background #21262d, alternating row stripes #161b22 / #0f1117, cell padding 8px 12px, and monospace font for parameter names.

Changelog page renders a vertical timeline using a 2px solid #30363d left border on a single centered column (max-width 720px), with version badges as inline-block pills: #58a6ff background for minor releases, #3fb950 for patch, #f85149 for breaking changes.

page_specs.getting-started: Presents a three-step onboarding flow: Install via npm (code block showing 'npm install ferroflux-sdk'), Initialize a sensor pipeline with a YAML snippet, and stream the first reading with a 12-line JavaScript example. A callout box in #21262d with a left border in #58a6ff highlights minimum system requirements: Node 18+, Linux kernel 5.15+, USB-HID or SPI interface.
page_specs.api-reference: Full REST and WebSocket endpoint reference organized in the left nav by resource group (Sensors, Streams, Calibration, Auth). Each endpoint section shows the HTTP method badge, URL pattern, a parameters table with columns Name/Type/Required/Description, and a sticky right-panel with a curl example and a JSON response sample. Includes 14 endpoints total across four resource groups.
page_specs.configuration: Documents the ferroflux.yaml configuration schema using a dense two-column table: left column shows the dot-notation key (e.g. 'pipeline.bufferSize'), right column shows type, default value, and a one-sentence description. Groups are separated by sticky sub-headers. A secondary section shows environment variable overrides in a monospace table with FERROFLUX_ prefixed names.
page_specs.changelog: Vertical timeline of 8 releases from v0.1.0 to v1.4.2 spanning 18 months, each entry showing the version pill, release date in #8b949e, a bold summary line, and a bulleted list of changes grouped under Added / Fixed / Breaking. Version v1.0.0 entry is visually larger with a horizontal rule and a migration note callout.
page_specs.troubleshooting: Three-column layout at the top showing quick-stat cards: 'Most Common Error', 'Avg Resolution Time', 'Open Issues'. Below, a filterable issue table (filter by category via sticky sidebar: Hardware, Auth, Streaming, Config) listing 12 known issues with columns Issue ID, Symptom, Cause, Resolution, and a Status badge (Resolved in #3fb950, Investigating in #e3b341, Wontfix in #8b949e).
